verses.text: From 'the end of the earth' David cries, asking to be led to 'the rock that is higher than I,' longing to abide in God's tabernacle for ever. Overwhelmed, he seeks a refuge above himself. When trouble is too high for us, we flee to the Rock that is higher still.

From 'the end of the earth' David cries, asking to be led to 'the rock that is higher than I,' longing to abide in God's tabernacle for ever. Overwhelmed, he seeks a refuge above himself. When trouble is too high for us, we flee to the Rock that is higher still.

From 'the end of the earth' David cries, asking to be led to 'the rock that is higher than I,' longing to abide in God's tabernacle for ever. Overwhelmed, he seeks a refuge above himself. When trouble is too high for us, we flee to the Rock that is higher still.

From 'the end of the earth' David cries, asking to be led to 'the rock that is higher than I,' longing to abide in God's tabernacle for ever. Overwhelmed, he seeks a refuge above himself. When trouble is too high for us, we flee to the Rock that is higher still.

God has heard his vows and given him the heritage of those who fear His name; the king shall abide before God for ever, so David will sing praise and pay his vows daily. Answered prayer renews devotion. Mercy received binds the heart to faithful, daily praise.
